title: hemionitis fractifera
intro: Hemionitis fractifera, also known as the Lizard's Tail or Lizard's Tail Fern, is a species of plant in the family Polypodiaceae, native to tropical Asia and the Pacific Islands. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Hemionitis fractifera include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Hemionitis fractifera can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, allowing for various forms of consumption to harness its medicinal properties.
